name: Contribution Score
summary: Scores each Deep Funding Portal user (by DID) from the comments they write.
description:
### What it does
Scores users based on the comments they write in DeepFunding.

### How it works
- Every Deep Funding Portal user is scored, keyed by their DID.
- Each valid comment starts with a base score.
- Upvotes add points and downvotes remove points.
- Comments on your own proposal or self-replies can get a penalty.
- A proposal owner upvote can add a bonus.
- Older comments can lose weight over time.

### Data source
Uses proposal, comment, user, and vote data from DeepFunding. Each comment's author is attributed to that user's DID.

### Output
- CSV file with `did` and `contribution_score`.
- JSON file with score details for comments and scored users.

inputs.label: Base Score Per Comment
inputs.description: Points each valid comment gets before votes, bonuses, penalties, and decay.

inputs.label: Upvote Weight
inputs.description: How much one upvote increases a comment score.

inputs.label: Downvote Weight
inputs.description: How much one downvote reduces a comment score.

inputs.label: Self-Interaction Penalty
inputs.description: Multiplier for comments on your own proposal or for self-replies. Use `1` for no penalty.

inputs.label: Proposal Owner Upvote Bonus
inputs.description: Multiplier when the proposal owner upvotes a comment. Use `1` for no bonus.

inputs.label: Lookback Window (Months)
inputs.description: Only comments from this number of past months are scored.

inputs.label: Monthly Decay Rate (%)
inputs.description: Percent removed from a comment score for each month of age. Use `0` for no decay.

outputs.label: Contribution Score (CSV)
outputs.description: CSV file with one row per user and the final contribution score.

columns.description: Final contribution score for the user.

outputs.label: Contribution Score Details (JSON)
outputs.description: JSON file with score details for comments and scored users.
